1	"""Terminal agent that answers questions across multiple MCP servers.
2	
3	Connects to two MCP servers simultaneously and merges their tools:
4	  - Qontextually (stdio) \u2014 internal graph: search_context, get_entity, get_provenance, list_entities_by_type, get_source
5	  - Tavily (streamable HTTP) \u2014 live web: tavily_search, tavily_extract, tavily_crawl, tavily_map, tavily_research
6	
7	OpenRouter drives the LLM (qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct by default).
8	rich.live renders tool calls, results, and citations in real time. Each
9	tool call is routed to the MCP server that owns it via a name\u2192session map.
10	
11	Invoke as:
12	    python -m lib.agent "Who manages the Phoenix project?"
13	    python -m lib.agent --no-web "Internal-only question"
14	
15	The agent loops: LLM picks a tool \u2192 we dispatch to the right MCP server \u2192
16	result goes back to LLM \u2192 repeat until LLM emits a final answer. Citations
17	in the final answer point to triple_ids + source_ids that exist in the DB
18	or (for web tools) to the returned URLs.
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import argparse
23	import asyncio
24	import json
25	import os
26	import sys
27	import time
28	from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
29	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
30	from typing import Any, Optional
31	
32	import requests
33	from dotenv import load_dotenv
34	from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters
35	from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client
36	from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
37	from rich.box import ROUNDED
38	from rich.console import Console, Group
39	from rich.live import Live
40	from rich.panel import Panel
41	from rich.spinner import Spinner
42	from rich.table import Table
43	from rich.text import Text
44	
45	
46	OPENROUTER_URL = os.environ.get(
47	    "QONTEXT_EXTRACT_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions"
48	)
49	API_KEY_ENV = "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
50	
51	
52	def _agent_model() -> str:
53	    return os.environ.get("QONTEXT_AGENT_MODEL", "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct")
54	
55	
56	def _agent_model_fallback() -> str:
57	    return os.environ.get("QONTEXT_AGENT_MODEL_FALLBACK", "openai/gpt-4o-mini")
58	MAX_TURNS = 8
59	TEMPERATURE = 0.1
60	MAX_TOKENS = 2048
61	
62	
63	SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You answer questions about a company called Inazuma.co using a knowledge graph plus live web search when needed.
64	
65	You have TWO classes of tools:
66	
67	INTERNAL GRAPH tools (start with search_context / get_entity / get_provenance / list_entities_by_type / get_source):
68	  - Use these FIRST for anything about Inazuma: people, projects, policies, emails, tickets, org structure.
69	  - Every fact here has a triple_id and source_id you can cite.
70	
71	EXTERNAL WEB tools (start with tavily_): tavily_search, tavily_extract, tavily_crawl, tavily_map, tavily_research
72	  - Use ONLY for facts the internal graph cannot know: current laws, industry standards, external benchmarks, definitions of general terms, market prices.
73	  - Never use web tools for Inazuma-internal facts; the internal graph is authoritative there.
74	
75	Decision procedure:
76	1. If the question mentions an Inazuma entity (a name, policy, project): start with search_context.
77	2. If search_context returns relevant hits, expand with get_entity and cite with get_provenance.
78	3. If the question needs external context (e.g. "does our policy match German law?"), call tavily_search AFTER you have gathered the internal side.
79	4. Stop as soon as you can answer. Do not call tools you do not need.
80	
81	Final answer format (plain text):
82	- Direct, 1-3 sentences.
83	- A "Citations:" section listing each fact:
84	    - Internal: "- <fact> (triple #N, source #M)"
85	    - Web:      "- <fact> (web: <url>)"
86	- If both sources contributed, cite both.
87	
88	If the graph has nothing and the question is about Inazuma, say "Inazuma's graph has no record of that" and stop. Do not invent. Do not fall back to the web for internal Inazuma questions.
89	"""
90	
91	
92	@dataclass
93	class AgentTrace:
94	    question: str
95	    model: str
96	    turns: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
97	    final_answer: Optional[str] = None
98	    elapsed_s: float = 0.0
99	    total_tokens: int = 0
100	    total_cost: float = 0.0
101	
102	    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
103	        return {
104	            "question": self.question,
105	            "model": self.model,
106	            "turns": self.turns,
107	            "final_answer": self.final_answer,
108	            "elapsed_s": self.elapsed_s,
109	            "total_tokens": self.total_tokens,
110	            "total_cost": self.total_cost,
111	        }
112	
113	
114	def _mcp_tool_to_openai(tool: Any) -> dict:
115	    return {
116	        "type": "function",
117	        "function": {
118	            "name": tool.name,
119	            "description": tool.description,
120	            "parameters": tool.inputSchema,
121	        },
122	    }
123	
124	
125	def _truncate(value: Any, limit: int = 300) -> str:
126	    s = str(value)
127	    return s if len(s) <= limit else s[:limit] + "\u2026"
128	
129	
130	def _call_openrouter(model: str, messages: list[dict], tools: list[dict]) -> dict:
131	    key = os.environ.get(API_KEY_ENV)
132	    if not key:
133	        raise RuntimeError(f"{API_KEY_ENV} not set")
134	    resp = requests.post(
135	        OPENROUTER_URL,
136	        headers={
137	            "Authorization": f"Bearer {key}",
138	            "Content-Type": "application/json",
139	            "HTTP-Referer": "https://github.com/b3ll9trix/qontextually",
140	            "X-Title": "Qontextually Agent",
141	        },
142	        json={
143	            "model": model,
144	            "messages": messages,
145	            "tools": tools,
146	            "tool_choice": "auto",
147	            "temperature": TEMPERATURE,
148	            "max_tokens": MAX_TOKENS,
149	        },
150	        timeout=90,
151	    )
152	    if resp.status_code >= 400:
153	        raise RuntimeError(f"OpenRouter HTTP {resp.status_code}: {resp.text[:300]}")
154	    return resp.json()
155	
156	
157	def _render(question: str, trace: AgentTrace, status: str) -> Panel:
158	    grp: list[Any] = []
159	    grp.append(Panel(Text(question, style="bold cyan"), title="Question", border_style="cyan", box=ROUNDED))
160	
161	    if trace.turns:
162	        tbl = Table(box=ROUNDED, expand=True, show_header=True, header_style="bold")
163	        tbl.add_column("#", width=3)
164	        tbl.add_column("Tool", style="magenta")
165	        tbl.add_column("Arguments", style="yellow")
166	        tbl.add_column("Result preview", style="green")
167	        for i, turn in enumerate(trace.turns, 1):
168	            args = turn.get("arguments") or {}
169	            args_s = ", ".join(f"{k}={_truncate(v, 40)}" for k, v in args.items())
170	            result_s = _truncate(turn.get("result_preview") or "", 90)
171	            tbl.add_row(str(i), turn.get("tool", "?"), args_s, result_s)
172	        grp.append(Panel(tbl, title="Tool calls", border_style="magenta", box=ROUNDED))
173	
174	    if trace.final_answer:
175	        grp.append(
176	            Panel(
177	                Text(trace.final_answer, style="white"),
178	                title=f"Answer  \u00b7  {trace.model}  \u00b7  {trace.elapsed_s:.1f}s  \u00b7  ${trace.total_cost:.4f}",
179	                border_style="green",
180	                box=ROUNDED,
181	            )
182	        )
183	    else:
184	        grp.append(Panel(Spinner("dots", text=status), border_style="dim"))
185	
186	    return Panel(Group(*grp), border_style="bright_black", box=ROUNDED)
187	
188	
189	async def _mount_internal(stack: AsyncExitStack) -> tuple[ClientSession, list[Any]]:
190	    """Spawn lib.mcp_server via stdio, initialize a session, return (session, tools)."""
191	    params = StdioServerParameters(
192	        command=".venv/bin/python",
193	        args=["-m", "lib.mcp_server"],
194	        cwd=os.getcwd(),
195	    )
196	    read, write = await stack.enter_async_context(stdio_client(params))
197	    session = await stack.enter_async_context(ClientSession(read, write))
198	    await session.initialize()
199	    tools = (await session.list_tools()).tools
200	    return session, tools
201	
202	
203	async def _mount_tavily(stack: AsyncExitStack) -> Optional[tuple[ClientSession, list[Any]]]:
204	    """Connect to Tavily's remote MCP via streamable HTTP. Returns None if no key."""
205	    key = os.environ.get("TAVILY_API_KEY")
206	    if not key:
207	        return None
208	    url = f"https://mcp.tavily.com/mcp/?tavilyApiKey={key}"
209	    try:
210	        streams = await stack.enter_async_context(streamablehttp_client(url))
211	        read, write = streams[0], streams[1]
212	        session = await stack.enter_async_context(ClientSession(read, write))
213	        await session.initialize()
214	        tools = (await session.list_tools()).tools
215	        return session, tools
216	    except Exception as exc:
217	        return None
218	
219	
220	def _preview_result(result_text: str) -> str:
221	    try:
222	        obj = json.loads(result_text)
223	    except Exception:
224	        return _truncate(result_text, 90)
225	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
226	        if "error" in obj:
227	            return obj["error"]
228	        if "total_hits" in obj:
229	            return f"{obj['total_hits']} hits"
230	        if "results" in obj and isinstance(obj["results"], list):
231	            return f"{len(obj['results'])} web results"
232	        if "name" in obj:
233	            return f"{obj['name']} ({obj.get('type','?')})"
234	        if "total" in obj:
235	            return f"{obj['total']} items"
236	    return _truncate(obj, 90)
237	
238	
239	async def _arun(question: str, verbose: bool = False, use_web: bool = True) -> AgentTrace:
240	    trace = AgentTrace(question=question, model=_agent_model())
241	    console = Console()
242	
243	    async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
244	        internal_session, internal_tools = await _mount_internal(stack)
245	
246	        session_by_tool: dict[str, ClientSession] = {t.name: internal_session for t in internal_tools}
247	        openai_tools = [_mcp_tool_to_openai(t) for t in internal_tools]
248	
249	        tavily_ok = False
250	        if use_web:
251	            mounted = await _mount_tavily(stack)
252	            if mounted is not None:
253	                tavily_session, tavily_tools = mounted
254	                for t in tavily_tools:
255	                    session_by_tool[t.name] = tavily_session
256	                    openai_tools.append(_mcp_tool_to_openai(t))
257	                tavily_ok = True
258	
259	        messages: list[dict] = [
260	            {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
261	            {"role": "user", "content": question},
262	        ]
263	        start = time.time()
264	        primary = _agent_model()
265	        fallback = _agent_model_fallback()
266	        model = primary
267	
268	        boot_status = f"Consulting {model}" + (" (internal + web)" if tavily_ok else " (internal only)")
269	
270	        with Live(_render(question, trace, boot_status + "\u2026"), console=console, refresh_per_second=8) as live:
271	            for turn_ix in range(MAX_TURNS):
272	                try:
273	                    resp = _call_openrouter(model, messages, openai_tools)
274	                except Exception as exc:
275	                    if model == primary:
276	                        model = fallback
277	                        trace.model = model
278	                        live.update(_render(question, trace, f"Primary failed, falling back to {model}\u2026"))
279	                        continue
280	                    trace.final_answer = f"Agent failed: {exc}"
281	                    live.update(_render(question, trace, ""))
282	                    break
283	
284	                usage = resp.get("usage", {}) or {}
285	                trace.total_tokens += usage.get("total_tokens") or 0
286	                trace.total_cost += usage.get("cost") or 0.0
287	
288	                msg = resp["choices"][0]["message"]
289	                tool_calls = msg.get("tool_calls") or []
290	                content = msg.get("content") or ""
291	
292	                messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": content, "tool_calls": tool_calls})
293	
294	                if not tool_calls:
295	                    trace.final_answer = content.strip() or "(empty answer)"
296	                    trace.elapsed_s = time.time() - start
297	                    live.update(_render(question, trace, ""))
298	                    break
299	
300	                for tc in tool_calls:
301	                    fn = tc["function"]
302	                    tool_name = fn["name"]
303	                    try:
304	                        arguments = json.loads(fn.get("arguments") or "{}")
305	                    except Exception:
306	                        arguments = {}
307	
308	                    source = "web" if tool_name.startswith("tavily") else "graph"
309	                    live.update(_render(question, trace, f"Calling {tool_name} ({source})\u2026"))
310	
311	                    session = session_by_tool.get(tool_name)
312	                    if session is None:
313	                        result_text = json.dumps({"error": f"unknown tool {tool_name}"})
314	                    else:
315	                        try:
316	                            result = await session.call_tool(tool_name, arguments)
317	                            result_text = result.content[0].text if result.content else "{}"
318	                        except Exception as exc:
319	                            result_text = json.dumps({"error": str(exc)})
320	
321	                    trace.turns.append({
322	                        "tool": tool_name,
323	                        "source": source,
324	                        "arguments": arguments,
325	                        "result_preview": _preview_result(result_text),
326	                        "result_full": result_text,
327	                    })
328	                    live.update(_render(question, trace, ""))
329	
330	                    messages.append({
331	                        "role": "tool",
332	                        "tool_call_id": tc["id"],
333	                        "content": result_text[:8000],
334	                    })
335	            else:
336	                trace.final_answer = "(reached max turns without final answer)"
337	                trace.elapsed_s = time.time() - start
338	
339	    if verbose:
340	        console.print(Panel(Text(json.dumps(trace.as_dict(), indent=2, default=str)[:4000], style="dim"), title="Trace", border_style="dim"))
341	
342	    return trace
343	
344	
345	def main() -> int:
346	    load_dotenv(".env")
347	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Qontextually Q&A agent")
348	    parser.add_argument("question", help="Question to ask the graph")
349	    parser.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Print full trace JSON")
350	    parser.add_argument("--save-trace", help="Save full trace to a JSON file")
351	    parser.add_argument("--no-web", action="store_true", help="Skip Tavily MCP (internal graph only)")
352	    parser.add_argument("--speak", action="store_true", help="Speak the final answer aloud via Gradium TTS")
353	    args = parser.parse_args()
354	
355	    trace = asyncio.run(_arun(args.question, verbose=args.verbose, use_web=not args.no_web))
356	
357	    if args.save_trace:
358	        with open(args.save_trace, "w") as f:
359	            json.dump(trace.as_dict(), f, indent=2, default=str)
360	        print(f"\n[saved trace to {args.save_trace}]")
361	
362	    if args.speak and trace.final_answer:
363	        from lib.voice import speak
364	        try:
365	            asyncio.run(speak(trace.final_answer))
366	        except Exception as exc:
367	            print(f"[TTS failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}]")
368	
369	    return 0 if trace.final_answer else 1
370	
371	
372	if __name__ == "__main__":
373	    sys.exit(main())
374

file.content:
"""Terminal agent that answers questions across multiple MCP servers.

Connects to two MCP servers simultaneously and merges their tools:
  - Qontextually (stdio) \u2014 internal graph: search_context, get_entity, get_provenance, list_entities_by_type, get_source
  - Tavily (streamable HTTP) \u2014 live web: tavily_search, tavily_extract, tavily_crawl, tavily_map, tavily_research

OpenRouter drives the LLM (qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct by default).
rich.live renders tool calls, results, and citations in real time. Each
tool call is routed to the MCP server that owns it via a name\u2192session map.

Invoke as:
    python -m lib.agent "Who manages the Phoenix project?"
    python -m lib.agent --no-web "Internal-only question"

The agent loops: LLM picks a tool \u2192 we dispatch to the right MCP server \u2192
result goes back to LLM \u2192 repeat until LLM emits a final answer. Citations
in the final answer point to triple_ids + source_ids that exist in the DB
or (for web tools) to the returned URLs.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import asyncio
import json
import os
import sys
import time
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Optional

import requests
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from mcp import ClientSession, StdioServerParameters
from mcp.client.stdio import stdio_client
from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client
from rich.box import ROUNDED
from rich.console import Console, Group
from rich.live import Live
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.spinner import Spinner
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text


OPENROUTER_URL = os.environ.get(
    "QONTEXT_EXTRACT_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions"
)
API_KEY_ENV = "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"


def _agent_model() -> str:
    return os.environ.get("QONTEXT_AGENT_MODEL", "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct")


def _agent_model_fallback() -> str:
    return os.environ.get("QONTEXT_AGENT_MODEL_FALLBACK", "openai/gpt-4o-mini")
MAX_TURNS = 8
TEMPERATURE = 0.1
MAX_TOKENS = 2048


SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You answer questions about a company called Inazuma.co using a knowledge graph plus live web search when needed.

You have TWO classes of tools:

INTERNAL GRAPH tools (start with search_context / get_entity / get_provenance / list_entities_by_type / get_source):
  - Use these FIRST for anything about Inazuma: people, projects, policies, emails, tickets, org structure.
  - Every fact here has a triple_id and source_id you can cite.

EXTERNAL WEB tools (start with tavily_): tavily_search, tavily_extract, tavily_crawl, tavily_map, tavily_research
  - Use ONLY for facts the internal graph cannot know: current laws, industry standards, external benchmarks, definitions of general terms, market prices.
  - Never use web tools for Inazuma-internal facts; the internal graph is authoritative there.

Decision procedure:
1. If the question mentions an Inazuma entity (a name, policy, project): start with search_context.
2. If search_context returns relevant hits, expand with get_entity and cite with get_provenance.
3. If the question needs external context (e.g. "does our policy match German law?"), call tavily_search AFTER you have gathered the internal side.
4. Stop as soon as you can answer. Do not call tools you do not need.

Final answer format (plain text):
- Direct, 1-3 sentences.
- A "Citations:" section listing each fact:
    - Internal: "- <fact> (triple #N, source #M)"
    - Web:      "- <fact> (web: <url>)"
- If both sources contributed, cite both.

If the graph has nothing and the question is about Inazuma, say "Inazuma's graph has no record of that" and stop. Do not invent. Do not fall back to the web for internal Inazuma questions.
"""


@dataclass
class AgentTrace:
    question: str
    model: str
    turns: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    final_answer: Optional[str] = None
    elapsed_s: float = 0.0
    total_tokens: int = 0
    total_cost: float = 0.0

    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "question": self.question,
            "model": self.model,
            "turns": self.turns,
            "final_answer": self.final_answer,
            "elapsed_s": self.elapsed_s,
            "total_tokens": self.total_tokens,
            "total_cost": self.total_cost,
        }


def _mcp_tool_to_openai(tool: Any) -> dict:
    return {
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": tool.name,
            "description": tool.description,
            "parameters": tool.inputSchema,
        },
    }


def _truncate(value: Any, limit: int = 300) -> str:
    s = str(value)
    return s if len(s) <= limit else s[:limit] + "\u2026"


def _call_openrouter(model: str, messages: list[dict], tools: list[dict]) -> dict:
    key = os.environ.get(API_KEY_ENV)
    if not key:
        raise RuntimeError(f"{API_KEY_ENV} not set")
    resp = requests.post(
        OPENROUTER_URL,
        headers={
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {key}",
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "HTTP-Referer": "https://github.com/b3ll9trix/qontextually",
            "X-Title": "Qontextually Agent",
        },
        json={
            "model": model,
            "messages": messages,
            "tools": tools,
            "tool_choice": "auto",
            "temperature": TEMPERATURE,
            "max_tokens": MAX_TOKENS,
        },
        timeout=90,
    )
    if resp.status_code >= 400:
        raise RuntimeError(f"OpenRouter HTTP {resp.status_code}: {resp.text[:300]}")
    return resp.json()


def _render(question: str, trace: AgentTrace, status: str) -> Panel:
    grp: list[Any] = []
    grp.append(Panel(Text(question, style="bold cyan"), title="Question", border_style="cyan", box=ROUNDED))

    if trace.turns:
        tbl = Table(box=ROUNDED, expand=True, show_header=True, header_style="bold")
        tbl.add_column("#", width=3)
        tbl.add_column("Tool", style="magenta")
        tbl.add_column("Arguments", style="yellow")
        tbl.add_column("Result preview", style="green")
        for i, turn in enumerate(trace.turns, 1):
            args = turn.get("arguments") or {}
            args_s = ", ".join(f"{k}={_truncate(v, 40)}" for k, v in args.items())
            result_s = _truncate(turn.get("result_preview") or "", 90)
            tbl.add_row(str(i), turn.get("tool", "?"), args_s, result_s)
        grp.append(Panel(tbl, title="Tool calls", border_style="magenta", box=ROUNDED))

    if trace.final_answer:
        grp.append(
            Panel(
                Text(trace.final_answer, style="white"),
                title=f"Answer  \u00b7  {trace.model}  \u00b7  {trace.elapsed_s:.1f}s  \u00b7  ${trace.total_cost:.4f}",
                border_style="green",
                box=ROUNDED,
            )
        )
    else:
        grp.append(Panel(Spinner("dots", text=status), border_style="dim"))

    return Panel(Group(*grp), border_style="bright_black", box=ROUNDED)


async def _mount_internal(stack: AsyncExitStack) -> tuple[ClientSession, list[Any]]:
    """Spawn lib.mcp_server via stdio, initialize a session, return (session, tools)."""
    params = StdioServerParameters(
        command=".venv/bin/python",
        args=["-m", "lib.mcp_server"],
        cwd=os.getcwd(),
    )
    read, write = await stack.enter_async_context(stdio_client(params))
    session = await stack.enter_async_context(ClientSession(read, write))
    await session.initialize()
    tools = (await session.list_tools()).tools
    return session, tools


async def _mount_tavily(stack: AsyncExitStack) -> Optional[tuple[ClientSession, list[Any]]]:
    """Connect to Tavily's remote MCP via streamable HTTP. Returns None if no key."""
    key = os.environ.get("TAVILY_API_KEY")
    if not key:
        return None
    url = f"https://mcp.tavily.com/mcp/?tavilyApiKey={key}"
    try:
        streams = await stack.enter_async_context(streamablehttp_client(url))
        read, write = streams[0], streams[1]
        session = await stack.enter_async_context(ClientSession(read, write))
        await session.initialize()
        tools = (await session.list_tools()).tools
        return session, tools
    except Exception as exc:
        return None


def _preview_result(result_text: str) -> str:
    try:
        obj = json.loads(result_text)
    except Exception:
        return _truncate(result_text, 90)
    if isinstance(obj, dict):
        if "error" in obj:
            return obj["error"]
        if "total_hits" in obj:
            return f"{obj['total_hits']} hits"
        if "results" in obj and isinstance(obj["results"], list):
            return f"{len(obj['results'])} web results"
        if "name" in obj:
            return f"{obj['name']} ({obj.get('type','?')})"
        if "total" in obj:
            return f"{obj['total']} items"
    return _truncate(obj, 90)


async def _arun(question: str, verbose: bool = False, use_web: bool = True) -> AgentTrace:
    trace = AgentTrace(question=question, model=_agent_model())
    console = Console()

    async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
        internal_session, internal_tools = await _mount_internal(stack)

        session_by_tool: dict[str, ClientSession] = {t.name: internal_session for t in internal_tools}
        openai_tools = [_mcp_tool_to_openai(t) for t in internal_tools]

        tavily_ok = False
        if use_web:
            mounted = await _mount_tavily(stack)
            if mounted is not None:
                tavily_session, tavily_tools = mounted
                for t in tavily_tools:
                    session_by_tool[t.name] = tavily_session
                    openai_tools.append(_mcp_tool_to_openai(t))
                tavily_ok = True

        messages: list[dict] = [
            {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
            {"role": "user", "content": question},
        ]
        start = time.time()
        primary = _agent_model()
        fallback = _agent_model_fallback()
        model = primary

        boot_status = f"Consulting {model}" + (" (internal + web)" if tavily_ok else " (internal only)")

        with Live(_render(question, trace, boot_status + "\u2026"), console=console, refresh_per_second=8) as live:
            for turn_ix in range(MAX_TURNS):
                try:
                    resp = _call_openrouter(model, messages, openai_tools)
                except Exception as exc:
                    if model == primary:
                        model = fallback
                        trace.model = model
                        live.update(_render(question, trace, f"Primary failed, falling back to {model}\u2026"))
                        continue
                    trace.final_answer = f"Agent failed: {exc}"
                    live.update(_render(question, trace, ""))
                    break

                usage = resp.get("usage", {}) or {}
                trace.total_tokens += usage.get("total_tokens") or 0
                trace.total_cost += usage.get("cost") or 0.0

                msg = resp["choices"][0]["message"]
                tool_calls = msg.get("tool_calls") or []
                content = msg.get("content") or ""

                messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": content, "tool_calls": tool_calls})

                if not tool_calls:
                    trace.final_answer = content.strip() or "(empty answer)"
                    trace.elapsed_s = time.time() - start
                    live.update(_render(question, trace, ""))
                    break

                for tc in tool_calls:
                    fn = tc["function"]
                    tool_name = fn["name"]
                    try:
                        arguments = json.loads(fn.get("arguments") or "{}")
                    except Exception:
                        arguments = {}

                    source = "web" if tool_name.startswith("tavily") else "graph"
                    live.update(_render(question, trace, f"Calling {tool_name} ({source})\u2026"))

                    session = session_by_tool.get(tool_name)
                    if session is None:
                        result_text = json.dumps({"error": f"unknown tool {tool_name}"})
                    else:
                        try:
                            result = await session.call_tool(tool_name, arguments)
                            result_text = result.content[0].text if result.content else "{}"
                        except Exception as exc:
                            result_text = json.dumps({"error": str(exc)})

                    trace.turns.append({
                        "tool": tool_name,
                        "source": source,
                        "arguments": arguments,
                        "result_preview": _preview_result(result_text),
                        "result_full": result_text,
                    })
                    live.update(_render(question, trace, ""))

                    messages.append({
                        "role": "tool",
                        "tool_call_id": tc["id"],
                        "content": result_text[:8000],
                    })
            else:
                trace.final_answer = "(reached max turns without final answer)"
                trace.elapsed_s = time.time() - start

    if verbose:
        console.print(Panel(Text(json.dumps(trace.as_dict(), indent=2, default=str)[:4000], style="dim"), title="Trace", border_style="dim"))

    return trace


def main() -> int:
    load_dotenv(".env")
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Qontextually Q&A agent")
    parser.add_argument("question", help="Question to ask the graph")
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Print full trace JSON")
    parser.add_argument("--save-trace", help="Save full trace to a JSON file")
    parser.add_argument("--no-web", action="store_true", help="Skip Tavily MCP (internal graph only)")
    parser.add_argument("--speak", action="store_true", help="Speak the final answer aloud via Gradium TTS")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    trace = asyncio.run(_arun(args.question, verbose=args.verbose, use_web=not args.no_web))

    if args.save_trace:
        with open(args.save_trace, "w") as f:
            json.dump(trace.as_dict(), f, indent=2, default=str)
        print(f"\n[saved trace to {args.save_trace}]")

    if args.speak and trace.final_answer:
        from lib.voice import speak
        try:
            asyncio.run(speak(trace.final_answer))
        except Exception as exc:
            print(f"[TTS failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}]")

    return 0 if trace.final_answer else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

content.text:
Here's the lay of the land and a demo plan you can run start-to-finish.

## What you've got

**Graph state** (live, in `db/qontextually.db`):
- 29,152 entities · 103,739 triples · 20,167 sources · 2,631 predicates · 117 pending conflicts
- Top entity types: Project, Document, Person, Organization, Meeting, Product, Policy

**Demo surfaces:**
| Surface | How | Needs key? |
|---|---|---|
| REST API (already running, PID 46426) | http://127.0.0.1:8000 — `/stats`, `/entities`, `/conflicts`, `/vocabulary/discovered`, `/graph/subgraph`, ... | no |
| Raw graph browser | `make ui` → http://localhost:8080 (sqlite-web) | no |
| Replay agent (recorded traces) | `.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent_replay data/demo_qa/q1_leave_policy.json` | **no** |
| Live terminal Q&A agent | `.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent "..."` | OpenRouter (✓ in `.env`) + optional Tavily (✓) |
| Voice in (STT) | `.venv/bin/python -m lib.voice --record 6 --pipe` | Gradium (✓) |
| Voice out (TTS) | `... -m lib.agent "Q" --speak` | Gradium (✓) |
| MCP server for any client | `.venv/bin/python -m lib.mcp_server` | no |
| Live file drop | `.venv/bin/python -m lib.ingest --tier 1 --max 3 --no-skip` | OpenRouter |

All your `.env` keys are populated (OpenRouter, Gradium, Tavily, embeddings).

## Suggested 5-minute demo flow

1. **Frame the problem** — README's "The problem" paragraph. 30s.
2. **Show the raw graph** — `make ui` → browse `entities`, `triples`, `triple_sources`. Point out provenance is a column. 30s.
3. **Show the REST/HITL surface** — open http://127.0.0.1:8000/stats and `/conflicts?limit=3` to show the human review queue is real. 30s.
4. **Live agent answer with citations** — `.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent "What does the Leave Policy say about vacation entitlements?" --no-web`. Watch the rich.live tool calls; final answer cites `triple #N, source #M`. 90s.
5. **Replay (no-API) version** — `.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent_replay data/demo_qa/q1_leave_policy.json` — same UX, free, repeatable. 30s.
6. **Polarity caveat** — show `vocabulary_discovered` (e.g. `seeks_advice`/`offers_advice` at 0.932 cosine) → README's negation section. 60s.

## On the voice transcription

You hit "Wortless Leaf Policy" / "Seabrook Vacation Entitlements" — Gradium's `default` model is misfiring on domain terms ("Workless"/"Worklessleave", "Inazuma", policy names). Three options, easiest first:

- **Skip live mic for the demo.** Use `--file data/demo_audio/q1_leave.wav` (you already have a clean pre-recorded sample) or just type the question. Safer under hack-day audio conditions.
- **Pre-record the demo questions** in a quiet spot once and replay them via `--file`.
- **Improve mic capture** — `arecord` is grabbing whatever ALSA finds first. If the room is loud or the mic is far, transcription will keep degrading. Want me to add a `--device` flag and list available devices so you can pick a closer/better one?

Want me to do a dry-run of any step now (e.g. fire the live agent question and confirm it returns with proper citations)?

# ============================================================================
# Makefile for the Qontextually extraction pipeline
# ============================================================================
# Author: Reshma Suresh (https://github.com/b3ll9trix)
# ============================================================================

.PHONY: setup migrate run api api-stop clean deep-clean all install-uv

# Define the sandbox environment
VENV = .venv
PYTHON = $(VENV)/bin/python

# Dynamically locate uv, or set the default path where the installer puts it
UV := $(shell command -v uv 2> /dev/null || echo $(HOME)/.local/bin/uv)

# The default command when someone just types 'make'
all: setup migrate run

# Rule to auto-install uv if it is completely missing from the system
install-uv:
	@if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ ! -f "$(UV)" ]; then \
		echo "🚀 'uv' not found. Auto-installing Astral uv (Lightning-fast package manager)..."; \
		curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh; \
	fi

# Create the venv exactly once. Do NOT depend on requirements.txt here —
# venv creation is orthogonal to dependency sync.
$(VENV)/pyvenv.cfg: | install-uv
	@echo "⚡ Building Python sandbox with uv..."
	$(UV) venv $(VENV)

# Re-sync dependencies whenever requirements.txt changes. The stamp file lets
# Make tell whether the last sync matches the current reqs, without touching
# venv-created files.
$(VENV)/.deps.stamp: requirements.txt | $(VENV)/pyvenv.cfg
	@echo "📦 Installing dependencies at lightspeed..."
	$(UV) pip install -p $(PYTHON) -r requirements.txt
	@touch $@

setup: $(VENV)/.deps.stamp
migrate: setup
	@echo "🗄️ Applying database migrations..."
	$(PYTHON) db/setup.py --db db/qontextually.db --migrations migrations

run: migrate
	@echo "🚀 Starting the Qontextually extraction pipeline..."
ui: setup
	@echo "🌐 Launching the Qontext Virtual File System (Database Browser)..."
	$(VENV)/bin/sqlite_web db/qontextually.db

api: migrate
	@echo "🔌 Starting Qontextually API on http://127.0.0.1:8000 ..."
	@bash scripts/start_api.sh

api-stop:
	@echo "🛑 Stopping API server ..."
	@pkill -9 -f "uvicorn lib.api" 2>/dev/null || true
	@echo "  done"
clean:
	@echo "🧹 Sweeping up the sandbox and database..."
	rm -rf $(VENV)
	rm -f qontextually.db
	rm -rf __pycache__
	@echo "✨ Project environment is completely clean."

deep-clean: clean
	@echo "🧨 Removing Astral uv from the system..."
	rm -f $(UV)
	@echo "💥 All traces of uv have been removed."

lines: -.PHONY: setup migrate run api api-stop clean deep-clean all install-uv
+.PHONY: setup migrate run api api-stop agent agent-replay clean deep-clean all install-uv

content.text:
The standard CLI form is the cleanest then — Make doesn't pass `--flags` through naturally. Just run it directly:

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent "What does the Leave Policy say about vacation entitlements?"
.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent "Who manages the Phoenix project?" --no-web
.venv/bin/python -m lib.agent "What does the Leave Policy say?" --no-web --speak
```

Flags supported by `lib.agent`:
- `--no-web` — skip Tavily, internal graph only
- `--speak` — speak the answer via Gradium TTS
- `--verbose` — print full trace JSON
- `--save-trace PATH` — save trace to file

Want me to drop a tiny `scripts/agent.sh` wrapper so you can type `./scripts/agent.sh "Q" --no-web --speak` (shorter than the python -m form), or leave it as-is?

content.text:
Quick constraint to flag: `-s` is Make's own silent flag, so `make ask "Q" -s` silences Make instead of reaching the agent. Same problem with most short flags — Make eats them before the recipe runs.

Two options that get you that exact ergonomics:

**A) `./ask "Q" -s -nw`** — drop a small shell script at the repo root. Same tokens you wanted, just no `make` prefix.

**B) `make ask Q="..." S=1 NW=1`** — pure Make, but the flags become env-style, not the `-s -nw` you sketched.

I'd go with A — closer to what you asked for. Sound good? If yes I'll add `./ask`.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Ask the Qontextually terminal agent.
#   ./ask "What does the Leave Policy say?"
#   ./ask "Who manages Phoenix?" -nw       # internal graph only (no web)
#   ./ask "What is our PTO policy?" -s     # speak the answer
#   ./ask "..." -s -nw                     # both
set -eu
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

flags=()
question=""
for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    -s|--speak)   flags+=("--speak") ;;
    -nw|--no-web) flags+=("--no-web") ;;
    -*)           echo "unknown flag: $arg (supported: -s/--speak, -nw/--no-web)" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    *)            if [ -n "$question" ]; then echo "multiple questions given" >&2; exit 2; fi
                  question="$arg" ;;
  esac
done

if [ -z "$question" ]; then
  echo "usage: ./ask \"question\" [-s] [-nw]" >&2
  exit 2
fi

exec .venv/bin/python -m lib.agent "${flags[@]}" "$question"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Replay a recorded Qontextually agent trace (no LLM cost, no API key needed).
#   ./replay                        # default: q1_leave_policy
#   ./replay q3                     # short id → data/demo_qa/q3*.json
#   ./replay q3_raj                 # also fine
#   ./replay path/to/trace.json     # explicit path
#   ./replay -a                     # replay every trace in data/demo_qa/
#   ./replay q3 -s                  # speak the answer
#   ./replay q3 -d 0.3              # set inter-tool delay (seconds)
set -eu
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

TRACE_DIR="data/demo_qa"
DEFAULT_ID="q1_leave_policy"

flags=()
target=""
expect_delay=0
for arg in "$@"; do
  if [ "$expect_delay" = "1" ]; then
    flags+=("--delay" "$arg"); expect_delay=0; continue
  fi
  case "$arg" in
    -a|--all)   flags+=("--all") ;;
    -s|--speak) flags+=("--speak") ;;
    -d|--delay) expect_delay=1 ;;
    -*)         echo "unknown flag: $arg (supported: -a/--all, -s/--speak, -d/--delay N)" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    *)          if [ -n "$target" ]; then echo "multiple targets given" >&2; exit 2; fi
                target="$arg" ;;
  esac
done

# --all needs a directory, not a file
if printf '%s\n' "${flags[@]}" | grep -qx -- "--all"; then
  target="${target:-$TRACE_DIR}"
else
  target="${target:-$DEFAULT_ID}"
  if [ ! -f "$target" ]; then
    match=$(ls "$TRACE_DIR"/${target}*.json 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
    if [ -z "$match" ]; then

      ls "$TRACE_DIR" >&2
      exit 2

fi

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Replay a recorded Qontextually agent trace (no LLM cost, no API key needed).
#   ./replay                        # default: q1_leave_policy
#   ./replay q3                     # short id → data/demo_qa/q3*.json
#   ./replay q3_raj                 # also fine
#   ./replay path/to/trace.json     # explicit path
#   ./replay -a                     # replay every trace in data/demo_qa/
#   ./replay q3 -s                  # speak the answer
#   ./replay q3 -d 0.3              # set inter-tool delay (seconds)
set -eu
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

TRACE_DIR="data/demo_qa"
DEFAULT_ID="q1_leave_policy"

flags=()
target=""
expect_delay=0
for arg in "$@"; do
  if [ "$expect_delay" = "1" ]; then
    flags+=("--delay" "$arg"); expect_delay=0; continue
  fi
  case "$arg" in
    -a|--all)   flags+=("--all") ;;
    -s|--speak) flags+=("--speak") ;;
    -d|--delay) expect_delay=1 ;;
    -*)         echo "unknown flag: $arg (supported: -a/--all, -s/--speak, -d/--delay N)" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    *)          if [ -n "$target" ]; then echo "multiple targets given" >&2; exit 2; fi
                target="$arg" ;;
  esac
done

# --all needs a directory, not a file
if printf '%s\n' "${flags[@]}" | grep -qx -- "--all"; then
  target="${target:-$TRACE_DIR}"
else
  target="${target:-$DEFAULT_ID}"
  if [ ! -f "$target" ]; then
    match=$(ls "$TRACE_DIR"/${target}*.json 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
    if [ -z "$match" ]; then
      echo "no trace matching '$target' in $TRACE_DIR" >&2
      ls "$TRACE_DIR" >&2
      exit 2
    fi
    target="$match"
  fi
fi

exec .venv/bin/python -m lib.agent_replay "${flags[@]}" "$target"

content.text:
Done. `./replay` mirrors `./ask`'s style:

- `./replay` — runs `q1_leave_policy` by default
- `./replay q3` — short id resolves to `data/demo_qa/q3*.json`
- `./replay path/to/trace.json` — explicit path also works
- `./replay -a` — replay every trace in sequence
- `./replay q3 -s` — speak the answer
- `./replay q3 -d 0.3` — delay between tool calls

Unknown flags or missing traces error out with the available options listed.

message.content: What do you mean by trace here?

content.text:
A **trace** is a recording of one full agent run, saved as JSON. When you run `./ask "..."`, the agent does this loop:

1. Sends the question + tool list to the LLM
2. LLM picks an MCP tool (e.g. `search_context`) → agent dispatches it → result goes back to LLM
3. Repeat until the LLM emits a final answer with citations

A trace captures **every step of that**: the question, which model was used, every tool call (name + arguments + result preview + full result), the final answer, elapsed time, tokens, cost. You can see one in `data/demo_qa/q1_leave_policy.json` — earlier I read it, the structure is roughly:

```json
{
  "question": "What does the Leave Policy say about vacation entitlements?",
  "model": "qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct",
  "turns": [
    { "tool": "search_context", "arguments": {...}, "result_full": "..." },
    { "tool": "get_source",     "arguments": {...}, "result_full": "..." },
    { "tool": "get_entity",     ... },
    { "tool": "get_provenance", ... }
  ],
  "final_answer": "...with Citations: triple #N, source #M",
  "elapsed_s": 8.4,
  "total_cost": 0.0021
}
```

You generate one with `./ask "Q" --save-trace path.json` (the underlying flag — I didn't expose it through `./ask` yet).

**Why replay matters for the demo:** `./replay` re-executes the recorded tool calls **live against the current graph** (so provenance numbers stay real and verifiable) but plays back the **recorded LLM answer** without calling OpenRouter. That means:

- No API key needed → safe for jurors / offline rooms
- Zero cost per run → can demo on repeat
- Deterministic timing → no LLM-latency surprises mid-presentation
- Still proves the graph is real → tool calls hit the actual SQLite DB

Five traces ship in `data/demo_qa/` (leave policy, policy listing, person multi-hop, IT asset, compliance) covering the main demo scenarios.

  "http://127.0.0.1:8000/conflicts/14" \

done

content.text:
Findings on the live API:

| Endpoint | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /conflicts` | ✅ 200 | 117 items |
| `GET /conflicts?status=pending` | ✅ 200 | same 117 (all are pending) |
| `GET /conflicts?status=resolved` | ✅ 200 | empty — none have been resolved yet |
| `GET /conflicts/14` | ❌ 404 | **no single-conflict GET handler exists in `lib/api.py`** |
| `GET /conflicts/14/resolve` | ❌ 405 | `/resolve` is POST-only |

So the list works, but there's a real gap: no `GET /conflicts/{id}` to fetch one conflict's detail. If your UI links from the list to a detail page, it'll 404.

Which are you hitting? Two likely things:
1. **You want `GET /conflicts/{id}`** → I can add it (returns the same shape as one item from the list).
2. **`POST /conflicts/{id}/resolve` is failing** → it needs a JSON body `{"winner": "a"|"b"|"both"|"neither", "note": "..."}`. Want me to test it with a real body?

Tell me which one and I'll fix it.

content.text:
The API is actually clean — I checked and every one of the 117 conflicts returns a fully-populated payload (`candidate_a`/`candidate_b` both present, every `sources` array non-empty, every `source.authority` populated since it defaults to 0.5 for unknown types). CORS is wide open (`*`). So the API isn't returning anything that should make `.authority` undefined.

The error is on the UI side. Most likely cause given what's missing on the API:

**The UI is probably calling `GET /conflicts/{id}` for a detail view.** That endpoint doesn't exist (returns 404 with `{"detail":"Not Found"}`). The UI then renders the error response as if it were a conflict — so `data.candidate_a` is `undefined`, and `data.candidate_a.sources[0].authority` blows up exactly with the message you're seeing.

Two questions to confirm:

1. **What action triggers the error?** Page load of `/conflicts`, or clicking into a single conflict?
2. **Open browser devtools → Network tab → reproduce the error.** Which API URL has a non-200 response?

If it's #1 (clicking into a detail page), I can add `GET /conflicts/{id}` to `lib/api.py` in 30 seconds — it returns the same per-item shape the list already produces. Want me to do that?

message.content:
This is the prompt I posted onto Lovable for the UI
I need you to build a web UI for Qontextually, a human-in-the-loop review console for a knowledge-graph system. The graph extracts entities and facts from enterprise data (emails, HR records, policies, tickets, CRM). The UI lets a human reviewer inspect the graph and resolve ambiguous cases the automated system flags.
Stack: React + Vite + TypeScript + TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui (Lovable default). Use @tanstack/react-query for data fetching. Use react-force-graph-3d for the 3D visualization. Do NOT include authentication — this is an internal tool running locally.
Backend URL: http://localhost:8000 (configurable via VITE_API_URL env var, default http://localhost:8000). All requests go to this base URL.
---
The API (authoritative contract — do not invent fields)
Treat this section as the source of truth. Every field name, every type, every endpoint shape is fixed. Use these exactly.
GET /health
Returns { "status": "ok", "db_path": "db/qontextually.db" }. Used for a small status indicator in the app header.
GET /stats
Dashboard counters.
{
  "entities_total": 29152,
  "entities_by_type": [
    { "type": "Person", "count": 12450 },
    { "type": "Organization", "count": 3200 }
  ],
  "triples_total": 103737,
  "sources_total": 40138,
  "predicates_total": 2631,
  "predicates_seeded": 14,
  "predicates_auto_canonical": 2613,
  "predicates_merged": 4,
  "conflicts_pending": 0,
  "vocabulary_pending_review": 461,
  "avg_sources_per_triple": 1.58,
  "last_extraction_at": "2026-04-26T03:31:12Z"
}
GET /conflicts?status=pending&limit=50&offset=0
Paginated list of pending conflicts. A conflict happens when a functional predicate (like has_title) has two different values for the same entity from different sources.
{
  "total": 0,
  "items": [
    {
      "conflict_id": 12,
      "conflict_type": "fact",
      "subject_entity": {
        "id": "e_ec0c4bb0e690",
        "name": "Raj Patel",
        "type": "Person"
      },
      "predicate": "has_title",
      "candidate_a": {
        "triple_id": 4722,
        "value": "Director",
        "object_is_entity": false,
        "sources": [
          {
            "source_id": 4332,
            "document_path": "sample_dataset/.../employees.json",
            "source_type": "hr",
            "authority": 1.0,
            "extracted_at": "2026-04-26T00:15:12Z",
            "snippet": "...Raj Patel is a highly experienced Director in the Engineering category..."
          }
        ],
        "score_breakdown": { "authority": 1.0, "confidence": 1.0, "recency": 0.82, "total": 0.82 }
      },
      "candidate_b": {
        "triple_id": 4901,
        "value": "Senior Director",
        "object_is_entity": false,
        "sources": [
          {
            "source_id": 5201,
            "document_path": "sample_dataset/.../emails.json",
            "source_type": "email",
            "authority": 0.4,
            "extracted_at": "2026-04-26T00:18:44Z",
            "snippet": "Dear Senior Director Raj Patel, thank you for..."
          }
        ],
        "score_breakdown": { "authority": 0.4, "confidence": 1.0, "recency": 0.85, "total": 0.34 }
      },
      "auto_resolution_hint": {
        "winner": "a",
        "reason": "HR authority (1.0) substantially higher than email (0.4); score delta 0.48"
      },
      "created_at": "2026-04-26T00:18:44Z"
    }
  ]
}
POST /conflicts/{conflict_id}/resolve
Body: { "winner": "a" | "b" | "neither", "note": "optional reviewer note" }. Returns the updated conflict row with status: "human_resolved" and resolved_at.
GET /vocabulary/discovered?limit=50&offset=0&min_occurrences=1
Auto-discovered predicates that are candidates for review. The backend has already run tier-2 embedding resolution; anything returned here is in the human-review band (cosine 0.75–0.95) or genuinely novel.
{
  "total": 461,
  "items": [
    {
      "predicate": "has_skill",
      "occurrence_count": 7508,
      "last_used": "2026-04-26T03:30:54Z",
      "sample_triples": [
        { "subject_name": "Raj Patel", "object": "Software Engineering" },
        { "subject_name": "Priya Singh", "object": "Data Science" }
      ],
      "nearest_canonical": {
        "predicate": "has_title",
        "cosine": 0.82,
        "occurrence_count": 5431
      },
      "description": "Auto-discovered from extractor"
    }
  ]
}
POST /vocabulary/{predicate}/merge
Body: { "into": "has_title", "reason": "optional" }. Merges the source predicate into the target. Returns { "triples_rewritten": 234, "merged_at": "..." }.
POST /vocabulary/{predicate}/promote
Body: { "is_functional": true, "description": "optional" }. Marks the predicate as canonical (removes from review queue). Returns the updated predicate row.
POST /vocabulary/{predicate}/dismiss
Body: { "reason": "optional" }. Removes from review queue without merging (keeps as canonical auto-discovered predicate).
GET /entities?type=Person&q=raj&limit=50&offset=0
Paginated search/filter. Supports optional type filter and q fuzzy-match against entity name and aliases.

---
## Screens to build
### Navigation / Layout
- **Sidebar** (left, 240px): Dashboard, Conflicts (with badge count), Vocabulary (with badge count), Entities, Sources, Graph. At the bottom: backend health dot (green/red) from `/health`.
- **Top bar**: "Qontextually" wordmark left, app version/env right.
- **Main area**: route content.
- Dark mode only. Use shadcn's default dark theme (slate background, sparing use of blue and emerald for accent).
- Use monospace font for IDs (`e_ec0c4bb0e690`, `triple #4722`), sans-serif for everything else.
### Screen 1 — Dashboard (`/`)
Top counters in a responsive grid: Entities, Triples, Sources, Predicates. Each counter is a card with the big number, the breakdown below (e.g. Predicates: "14 seeded, 2613 auto, 4 merged"), and `last_extraction_at` in a small timestamp at the bottom.
Below the counters, **two prominent action cards** side by side:
1. **Pending Conflicts** (red accent if count > 0): "N conflicts need your review → Review". Button takes you to `/conflicts`.
2. **Discovered Vocabulary** (amber accent): "N predicates awaiting promotion → Review". Takes you to `/vocabulary`.
Below that, a small "Entities by type" horizontal bar chart (use recharts). Then an "avg sources per triple" metric with a short caption: "The graph is densely provenanced — most facts have more than one supporting source."
All data from `GET /stats`. Poll every 30s.
### Screen 2 — Conflict Queue (`/conflicts`)
**List view** at top: table of pending conflicts with columns: Subject entity | Predicate | Candidate A (value preview) | Candidate B (value preview) | Auto-suggestion | Created. Click a row to open the detail pane on the right side.
**Detail pane** (right, fixed 50%+ width on desktop, full-screen on mobile): the meat of the HITL moment.
Show the subject entity card at top (name + type + `id` in monospace).
Then two side-by-side candidate panels labeled **A** and **B**. Each shows:
- The value (literal string or object entity) in a large card
- A score breakdown widget: three small bars for authority × confidence × recency, multiplied, total score
- The list of supporting sources: for each source, show `source_type` badge (color-coded: hr=blue, crm=indigo, policy=violet, ticket=orange, email=slate, chat=gray), `document_path` truncated, `extracted_at`, and an expandable "view snippet" that reveals the `snippet` text.
Below the two panels, an **auto-suggestion banner**: "Our scoring suggests **Candidate A** wins (reason: ${auto_resolution_hint.reason}). You can accept this, override, or reject both."
Action bar at the bottom of the detail: three primary buttons:
- **Accept A** (green)
- **Accept B** (orange)
- **Neither** (gray)
Plus an optional single-line text input "Note (optional)". Clicking any button sends `POST /conflicts/{id}/resolve` with the chosen winner, then moves to the next conflict in the queue.
Keyboard shortcuts: `A` → accept A, `B` → accept B, `N` → neither. Show these as tiny badges on each button.
Empty state: a cheerful checkmark illustration with text "No pending conflicts. The system is caught up."
### Screen 3 — Discovered Vocabulary (`/vocabulary`)
Two-pane layout. **Left**: list of predicates awaiting review, sortable by occurrence count (default descending). Each row: predicate name (monospace), usage count, nearest canonical + cosine score, a small color dot for similarity (green ≥0.9, amber 0.85–0.9, gray <0.85).
**Right**: detail for the selected predicate.
- Name (big, monospace)
- Usage count + description (if any)
- **Nearest canonical match**: card showing `{ predicate, cosine, occurrence_count }`. Subtitle: "Similarity is above review threshold but below auto-merge. Your call."
- **Sample triples**: render 3–5 real triples in a readable format: `subject_name — predicate → object` (with object being the value from sample_triples[].object)
- Action bar at bottom:
  - **Merge into canonical** (primary) — with a subtle dropdown to override the target predicate (defaults to `nearest_canonical.predicate`). On click: `POST /vocabulary/{predicate}/merge` with `into` set.
  - **Promote as canonical** (secondary) — opens a small modal: "Mark as functional?" checkbox + description field. On submit: `POST /vocabulary/{predicate}/promote`.
  - **Dismiss** (tertiary, ghost style) — calls `POST /vocabulary/{predicate}/dismiss`.
Design callout above the list: a small banner explaining why 0.85+ pairs are here — exact text to show: *"These predicates scored between 0.75 and 0.95 cosine similarity to an existing canonical predicate. Our system treats this band as ambiguous: lexical variants (works_at ≈ works_for) usually cluster ≥ 0.95, but polarity-flipped pairs (seeks_advice vs offers_advice) can also score here. Your call is the final signal."*
### Screen 4 — Entity Browser (`/entities`)
Split layout: **left column (320px)** — search + filter + results list; **right** — entity detail.
Left column:
- Search input (debounced 300ms, updates `q` param on `/entities`)
- Type filter chips: All, Person, Organization, Project, Ticket, Policy, Document, Event, Product, Meeting, Message (click to toggle one active type)
- Results as a virtual-scrolled list (use `react-window` or similar for perf). Each row: name, type badge, small preview of properties (e.g. "Engineering · Director"), triple count.
- Pagination or infinite scroll with `react-query`'s `useInfiniteQuery`.
Right column (when an entity is selected, call `GET /entities/{id}`):
- Header: name (big), type badge, id (monospace small)
- Properties table: key / value pairs in a clean grid
- Aliases: chips with `alias_type` as label, asterisk for `is_primary`
- Tabs for "Outgoing (N)" and "Incoming (N)" triples. Each list shows predicate + object, clicking the triple opens a provenance drawer that calls `GET /triples/{id}/provenance`.
- A button in the header: "Open in Graph View" — navigates to `/graph?center={id}`.
### Screen 5 — Source Browser (`/sources`)
Table layout with filters across the top: `source_type` dropdown (all / hr / crm / policy / ticket / email / chat / unknown), date range picker (on `extracted_at`).
Table columns: Source type badge | Document path (truncated, full path on hover) | Extracted at | Triple count | Entity count | Actions.
Click a row to open a modal with the full source view from `GET /sources/{id}`: complete `raw_text` in a scrollable code-style pane on the left (monospace, whitespace-pre), and on the right, two lists: "Contributed entities" and "Contributed triples" — each item a clickable link back to the Entity Browser.
Pagination at the bottom.
### Screen 6 — Graph Visualization (`/graph`)
A 3D force-directed graph using `react-force-graph-3d`.
**Critical constraints** (tell Lovable exactly this):
- The backend returns at most **500 nodes**. Do not attempt to render more. If user requests more detail, they should re-center on a specific entity.
- The default view (no `center` param) shows the 300 most-connected entities as an overview.
- Users can type an entity name into a search box; picking a result sets `center` and re-fetches.
- Node color by `type` (consistent with the entity-type badge colors used elsewhere).
- Node size proportional to `degree` (clamp: min 4, max 20).
- Edge thickness proportional to `source_count` (clamp: min 1, max 5) — this visually shows multi-source provenance.
- On node hover: tooltip with name, type, degree.
- On node click: open a panel on the right with the entity card (minimal version: name, type, properties) and a button "Inspect in Entity Browser" that navigates to `/entities?q={name}`.
- On edge hover: tooltip with predicate and source_count.
Controls overlay (top-right corner, floating):
- Search input: "Center on entity..."
- Depth selector: 1, 2, 3 (default 2)
- Max nodes selector: 100, 300, 500 (default 300)
- "Reset camera" button
Performance note for Lovable: use `react-force-graph-3d`'s `cooldownTicks={100}` to stop simulation once stable. Don't re-mount the graph on every filter change — use its `graphData` prop.
Empty state if no data: a clean panel explaining: "The graph is empty or the backend is unreachable. Check health indicator."
---
Styling and UX rules
- Dark mode only, using shadcn/ui's default dark theme.
- Density: moderate — prefer whitespace over cramming, but don't waste vertical space.
- Monospace (font-mono): all IDs (e_ec0c4bb0e690, triple #4722, source #4332), all predicate names, all document paths. Everything else sans-serif.
- Color palette for source types: consistent across all screens. hr=blue-500, crm=indigo-500, policy=violet-500, ticket=orange-500, email=slate-500, chat=gray-500, unknown=neutral-500.
- Color palette for entity types: Person=emerald-500, Organization=sky-500, Project=amber-500, Policy=violet-500, Ticket=orange-500, everything else=gray-400.
- Empty states are friendly, not barren. Explain why the list is empty and what would populate it.
- Loading states: skeleton components (shadcn/ui Skeleton), not spinners.
- Error states: a small red banner at the top of the screen saying what failed and a "Retry" button. Never fail silently.
- Keyboard shortcuts for Conflict Queue (A/B/N) and Vocabulary (M to merge, P to promote, D to dismiss). Show a small "?" button in the top bar that opens a shortcut legend modal.
Things NOT to do
- Do NOT add authentication, login, or user accounts. Local tool.
- Do NOT add write endpoints I haven't specified (creating entities by hand, deleting triples, etc.). Read + resolve + merge + promote + dismiss only.
- Do NOT render graphs over 500 nodes. Do NOT try to fetch all entities in one call for the graph.
- Do NOT add a chat/question interface. That's a separate program (a terminal agent).
- Do NOT use absolute imports from @/* aliases if Lovable hasn't configured them — use relative paths.
- Do NOT invent field names not in the API contract above.
Demo script context (for shaping emphasis)
The UI will be demoed live for judges at a hackathon. The presentation flow:
1. Open Dashboard — judge sees the scale (29k entities, 103k triples) and the two "pending review" action cards.
2. Click Conflicts → resolve one conflict visually (A/B/Neither).
3. Click Vocabulary → merge a synonym predicate into a canonical one.
4. Click Graph → a colored 3D force graph spins, judges see multi-source-provenance-as-thick-edges.
Optimize the Dashboard, Conflicts, and Vocabulary screens for visual impressiveness and clarity. Graph is the "flashy close." Entities and Sources are inspection tools, less demo-critical but still clean.
Build this as one coherent app
Not a collection of disconnected pages. Shared sidebar, shared top bar, shared theme, shared color conventions. React-query-based data fetching everywhere with consistent error handling. Pagination everywhere lists are long.

lastPrompt: This is the prompt I posted onto Lovable for the UI I need you to build a web UI for Qontextually, a human-in-the-loop review console for a knowledge-graph system. The graph extracts entities and fact…

content.text:
Found it. **Contract mismatch between your spec and `lib/api.py`.**

Your Lovable spec says each candidate has its own nested `score_breakdown`:

```json
"candidate_a": {
  "triple_id": ..., "value": ..., "object_is_entity": ..., "sources": [...],
  "score_breakdown": { "authority": 1.0, "confidence": 1.0, ... }   // ← UI expects this
}
```

But the actual API returns `score_breakdown_a` / `score_breakdown_b` at the **top level** of the conflict, and the candidates have no `score_breakdown` field. The UI destructures `const d = candidate_a.score_breakdown` → `d` is `undefined` → `d.authority` crashes with exactly the error you got.

I also need to check whether other endpoints have the same drift before fixing. Two minutes.

@app.get("/conflicts")
def list_conflicts(
    status: str = Query("pending"),
    limit: int = Query(50, ge=1, le=500),
    offset: int = Query(0, ge=0),
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    conn = get_connection()
    try:
        total = conn.execute(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conflicts WHERE status = ?", (status,)
        ).fetchone()[0]

        items = []
        rows = list(
            conn.execute(
                """
                SELECT c.id, c.conflict_type, c.triple_a_id, c.triple_b_id,
                       c.entity_a_id, c.entity_b_id, c.scores_json, c.created_at,
                       c.winner_ref, c.resolution_note
                FROM conflicts c
                WHERE c.status = ?
                ORDER BY c.created_at DESC
                LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
                """,
                (status, limit, offset),
            )
        )

        for c in rows:
            scores = _json_or_empty(c["scores_json"])
            item = {
                "conflict_id": c["id"],
                "conflict_type": c["conflict_type"],
                "created_at": c["created_at"],
                "winner_ref": c["winner_ref"],
                "resolution_note": c["resolution_note"],
            }

            if c["conflict_type"] == "fact":
                a = _candidate_detail(conn, c["triple_a_id"]) if c["triple_a_id"] else None
                b = _candidate_detail(conn, c["triple_b_id"]) if c["triple_b_id"] else None
                subject_entity = None
                if a and a["triple_id"]:
                    subj_row = conn.execute(
                        """
                        SELECT se.id, se.name, se.type FROM triples t JOIN entities se ON se.id = t.subject_id
                        WHERE t.id = ?
                        """,
                        (a["triple_id"],),
                    ).fetchone()
                    if subj_row:
                        subject_entity = dict(subj_row)
                predicate = None
                if c["triple_a_id"]:
                    pr = conn.execute(
                        "SELECT predicate FROM triples WHERE id = ?", (c["triple_a_id"],)
                    ).fetchone()
                    if pr:
                        predicate = pr["predicate"]
                item["subject_entity"] = subject_entity
                item["predicate"] = predicate
                item["candidate_a"] = a
                item["candidate_b"] = b
                item["score_breakdown_a"] = scores.get("a") if isinstance(scores, dict) else None
                item["score_breakdown_b"] = scores.get("b") if isinstance(scores, dict) else None
                item["auto_resolution_hint"] = scores.get("hint") if isinstance(scores, dict) else None

            else:
                item["entity_a_id"] = c["entity_a_id"]
                item["entity_b_id"] = c["entity_b_id"]

            items.append(item)

        return {"total": total, "items": items}
    finally:
        conn.close()


def _candidate_detail(conn: sqlite3.Connection, triple_id: int) -> dict:
    t = conn.execute(
        """
        SELECT t.id, t.predicate, t.object_id, t.object_value, t.object_is_entity,
               oe.name AS obj_name
        FROM triples t
        LEFT JOIN entities oe ON oe.id = t.object_id
        WHERE t.id = ?
        """,
        (triple_id,),
    ).fetchone()
    if t is None:
        return {}

    value = t["obj_name"] if t["object_is_entity"] else t["object_value"]
    sources = []
    for r in conn.execute(
        """
        SELECT s.id, s.document_path, s.source_type, s.extracted_at, s.raw_text,
               ts.confidence
        FROM triple_sources ts JOIN sources s ON s.id = ts.source_id
        WHERE ts.triple_id = ? ORDER BY ts.extracted_at ASC
        """,
        (triple_id,),
    ):
        sources.append(
            {
                "source_id": r["id"],
                "document_path": r["document_path"],
                "source_type": r["source_type"],
                "authority": SOURCE_AUTHORITY.get(r["source_type"], 0.5),
                "confidence": r["confidence"],
                "extracted_at": r["extracted_at"],
                "snippet": _snippet(r["raw_text"]),
            }
        )

    return {
        "triple_id": t["id"],
        "value": value,
        "object_is_entity": bool(t["object_is_entity"]),
        "sources": sources,
    }


class ConflictResolveBody(BaseModel):
    winner: str
    note: Optional[str] = None

message.content: Okay, now i need ideas on how to package the ui. I did that in lovable. How can I package that repo submission. Lovely connected to a new repo and not my existing backend one

content.text:
Four common ways to merge a separate Lovable UI into a backend repo for submission. I'd lean toward **#3 (copy source into `ui/`)** for a hackathon — judges get one clone, one command, and you keep the UI source for inspection.

| Option | Judge UX | Your effort | History preserved | Best when |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Git submodule** (`ui/` → Lovable repo) | needs `git clone --recurse-submodules`; Lovable's repo must stay public | low | yes (both repos independent) | you want both repos to keep evolving separately |
| **2. Git subtree** (squash UI into `ui/`) | single `git clone`; no submodule gotchas | medium (one-time `git subtree add`) | partial (subtree merge commit) | one-shot import with the option to pull updates later |
| **3. Copy source into `ui/`** + commit | single clone, single command | very low | no (you note "exported from Lovable" in README) | **hackathon submission** — simplest, judges don't care about UI git history |
| **4. Copy built `dist/` only**, served by FastAPI as static | single `pip install` + `make`; no Node needed at all | medium (need to build before commit) | no (no UI source in repo) | judges shouldn't need npm; risk: they can't see the source |

## Recommended layout (option 3)

```
qontextually/
├── lib/                # backend (existing)
├── ui/                 # ← Lovable export, full source
│   ├── src/
│   ├── package.json
│   └── vite.config.ts  # set proxy → http://127.0.0.1:8000
├── Makefile            # add `ui-dev` and `ui-build` targets
└── README.md           # updated demo flow
```

**Three things to wire up:**

1. **Vite proxy** so the UI calls `/conflicts` and Vite forwards to `:8000` — no CORS dance, no `.env` for the dev server. In `ui/vite.config.ts`:
   ```ts
   server: { proxy: { '^/(stats|conflicts|vocabulary|entities|sources|triples|graph|health)': 'http://127.0.0.1:8000' } }
   ```

2. **Makefile targets:**
   ```make
   ui-install: ui/node_modules
   ui/node_modules: ui/package.json
   	cd ui && npm install
   ui-dev: ui-install
   	cd ui && npm run dev      # opens http://localhost:5173
   ui-build: ui-install
   	cd ui && npm run build    # outputs ui/dist
   demo: api ui-dev             # one command for the demo
   ```

3. **README** — add a "Run the full demo" section: `make demo` → opens `http://localhost:5173`, backend on `:8000`. Replace the current "Lovable hosted UI" mention.

## Optional polish for judges who don't have Node

Add a third path: `make ui-build` once locally, commit the `ui/dist/` output, and have FastAPI mount it:

```python
# in lib/api.py
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
if Path("ui/dist").exists():
    app.mount("/", StaticFiles(directory="ui/dist", html=True), name="ui")
```

Now `make api` alone serves both the API and the UI on `:8000`. Judges with no Node still get the demo.

## Process for option 3

```bash
cd /home/b3ll9trix/code/bbh/qontextually
git clone <lovable-repo-url> /tmp/lovable-ui
rsync -av --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.git /tmp/lovable-ui/ ui/
echo "node_modules\ndist" > ui/.gitignore
git add ui Makefile README.md
git commit -m "ui: vendor Lovable export into ui/ for one-clone demo"
```

Want me to do that now? I'd need the Lovable repo URL (or you can `cp -r` it from wherever it lives locally).

message.content: I mean the entire app the entire app which involves other endpoints as well. that is a different repo

content.text:
Got it — the UI hits the whole API surface (conflicts, vocabulary, entities, sources, graph, …), so the two repos are tightly coupled. Two clean ways to ship that as one submission:

## A) Vendor the UI into this repo (recommended)

`ui/` becomes a directory in **this** repo. One clone, one command. The Lovable repo keeps existing — you just snapshot it.

```bash
git clone <lovable-repo-url> /tmp/ui-src
rsync -av --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.git /tmp/ui-src/ ui/
```

Then in this repo:
- `ui/.gitignore` → `node_modules/`, `dist/`
- `ui/vite.config.ts` → proxy `/stats /conflicts /vocabulary /entities /sources /triples /graph /health` → `http://127.0.0.1:8000`
- Makefile gains `ui-dev`, `ui-build`, `demo`
- README's demo section becomes: `make api` (terminal 1) + `make ui-dev` (terminal 2) → http://localhost:5173

Pros: judges see all the code together. One clone. Backend + UI versions move in lockstep.
Cons: UI git history doesn't come with it (note "exported from Lovable on YYYY-MM-DD" in `ui/README.md`).

## B) Two repos + a thin "meta" repo with docker-compose

A third repo, ~5 files: `docker-compose.yml`, `README.md`, an `.env.example`, a `Makefile` with `make up`. The compose file builds both repos as services. Judges clone the meta repo and run `docker compose up`.

Pros: each source repo stays as-is, no vendoring. Cleanest separation if you'll keep developing both.
Cons: more moving parts; judges need Docker; you have to write the Dockerfiles for both repos.

## My take for a hackathon

**Go with A.** Hackathon judges don't grade you on git submodule purity — they grade on "did it run when I cloned it." Vendoring is one commit, judges get a single command, and the UI source stays inspectable. Add a one-line credit in `ui/README.md` pointing back to the Lovable repo URL for transparency.

If you want, paste the Lovable repo URL (or `cp` it locally somewhere I can read) and I'll wire up `ui/`, the Vite proxy, the Makefile targets, and the README update in one pass.

input.content:
# Qontextually UI

Human-in-the-loop review console for the Qontextually graph. Built with TanStack Start (React + Vite) + TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui. Talks to the FastAPI backend in `../lib/api.py`.

Originally generated in [Lovable](https://lovable.dev) and lives at <https://github.com/b3ll9trix/qontextual-navigator>. This directory is a vendored snapshot for one-clone demo use; updates flow from the upstream repo.

## Run it

From the repo root:

```bash
make api      # backend on http://127.0.0.1:8000
make ui-dev   # UI on http://localhost:5173 (or whatever Vite picks)
```

Or directly inside this directory:

```bash
bun install && bun run dev   # bun preferred (matches bun.lockb)
# or
npm install && npm run dev   # npm fallback (package-lock.json is present)
```

Set `VITE_API_URL` to point at a non-default backend; defaults to `http://localhost:8000`.

## Screens

Dashboard · Conflicts · Vocabulary · Entities · Sources · Graph (3D force-directed). The full API contract this UI was built against is in the top-level `README.md`.

file.content:
# ============================================================================
# Makefile for the Qontextually extraction pipeline
# ============================================================================
# Author: Reshma Suresh (https://github.com/b3ll9trix)
# ============================================================================

.PHONY: setup migrate run api api-stop agent agent-replay clean deep-clean all install-uv

# Define the sandbox environment
VENV = .venv
PYTHON = $(VENV)/bin/python

# Dynamically locate uv, or set the default path where the installer puts it
UV := $(shell command -v uv 2> /dev/null || echo $(HOME)/.local/bin/uv)

# The default command when someone just types 'make'
all: setup migrate run

# Rule to auto-install uv if it is completely missing from the system
install-uv:
	@if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ ! -f "$(UV)" ]; then \
		echo "🚀 'uv' not found. Auto-installing Astral uv (Lightning-fast package manager)..."; \
		curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh; \
	fi

# Create the venv exactly once. Do NOT depend on requirements.txt here —
# venv creation is orthogonal to dependency sync.
$(VENV)/pyvenv.cfg: | install-uv
	@echo "⚡ Building Python sandbox with uv..."
	$(UV) venv $(VENV)

# Re-sync dependencies whenever requirements.txt changes. The stamp file lets
# Make tell whether the last sync matches the current reqs, without touching
# venv-created files.
$(VENV)/.deps.stamp: requirements.txt | $(VENV)/pyvenv.cfg
	@echo "📦 Installing dependencies at lightspeed..."
	$(UV) pip install -p $(PYTHON) -r requirements.txt
	@touch $@

setup: $(VENV)/.deps.stamp
migrate: setup
	@echo "🗄️ Applying database migrations..."
	$(PYTHON) db/setup.py --db db/qontextually.db --migrations migrations

run: migrate
	@echo "🚀 Starting the Qontextually extraction pipeline..."
ui: setup
	@echo "🌐 Launching the Qontext Virtual File System (Database Browser)..."
	$(VENV)/bin/sqlite_web db/qontextually.db

api: migrate
	@echo "🔌 Starting Qontextually API on http://127.0.0.1:8000 ..."
	@bash scripts/start_api.sh

api-stop:
	@echo "🛑 Stopping API server ..."
	@pkill -9 -f "uvicorn lib.api" 2>/dev/null || true
	@echo "  done"
clean:
	@echo "🧹 Sweeping up the sandbox and database..."
	rm -rf $(VENV)
	rm -f qontextually.db
	rm -rf __pycache__
	@echo "✨ Project environment is completely clean."

deep-clean: clean
	@echo "🧨 Removing Astral uv from the system..."
	rm -f $(UV)
	@echo "💥 All traces of uv have been removed."

input.old_string:
### HITL UI (Lovable)

The REST API under `lib/api.py` drives a Lovable-built review UI with screens for conflict resolution, predicate promotion, entity inspection, source browsing, a dashboard, and a 3D graph visualization. Run the backend:

```bash
make api          # starts http://127.0.0.1:8000 detached
make api-stop     # stop it
```

All endpoints documented inline. Smoke-test with `curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/health`.
input.new_string:
### HITL UI (vendored from Lovable)

The REST API under `lib/api.py` drives a review UI in `ui/` with screens for the dashboard, conflict resolution, predicate promotion, entity inspection, source browsing, and a 3D graph visualization. The UI is a TanStack Start (React + Vite) app originally generated in [Lovable](https://lovable.dev) and snapshotted here from <https://github.com/b3ll9trix/qontextual-navigator> for one-clone demo use.

```bash
make demo         # one command: API on :8000 (detached) + UI dev server (foreground)

# or run them separately:
make api          # backend on http://127.0.0.1:8000 detached
make ui-dev       # UI on http://localhost:5173
make api-stop     # stop the backend when done

# UI also builds standalone:
make ui-build     # static bundle in ui/dist/
```

`make ui-dev` uses [Bun](https://bun.sh) if installed (matches `ui/bun.lockb`); falls back to `npm` (which uses the committed `package-lock.json`). Override the API base with `VITE_API_URL` — defaults to `http://localhost:8000`.

All endpoints documented inline. Smoke-test the API alone with `curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/health`.
